title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. exile
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. exile, also known as the wood hawkweed, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) with yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The wood hawkweed is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting kidney function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or infused in tea to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
